patterns.notes: London and US moved in opposite directions (Whipsaw). US range expanded significantly vs London (Volatility Expansion). ORB 30m confirmed bearish. ORB 60m confirmed bullish. Failed ORB 30m favors bearish. Failed ORB 60m favors bearish. VWAP bearish reclaim/reject.

bias.explanation: Daily Bias is Bearish based on previous day (Bullish), Asia (Bearish), and London (Bearish) with bayesian equal-weight update. US Open Bias is Bearish (30m) / Bearish (60m). Overnight range position is Inside. Gap is small up. HTF slope bias is Neutral. Day type is Normal. Regime is MeanRevert / HMM Mixed. Event risk is none. Volatility regime is 'compressed', momentum and ROC aligned at -0.001, RVOL at 0.44. Kalman slope at -0.2484. Bayes prob 0.21 from equal weights. News tone is neutral and only adjusts confidence, not direction. Day impact: Session weighting favors larger-range sessions over quieter ones. ATR-scaled thresholds reduce false bias on quiet days. US open inside overnight range favors mean reversion early. Mean-revert regime: fade risk higher on extremes. Anchored VWAP aligned with daily bias for fair-value context. Price stretched far from anchored VWAP: mean-revert risk higher. Premarket bias (09:10 ET) uses overnight range, gap, VWAP, and premarket trend. Patterns aligned with other confluences and tilted the bias. HTF zones suggest Bearish bias (score -3.10; Bullish zones: 17, Bearish zones: 21.).
bias.vwap_comment: Price is below both daily and weekly VWAP (strong bearish posture). Suggestion: If price opens below daily VWAP, then a retracement up to daily VWAP is the first check; if it rejects, then continuation can push away from weekly VWAP resistance. If price opens above daily VWAP, then expect a deeper pullback toward weekly VWAP.
bias.news_comment: ForexFactory: neutral (No major high-impact events dominating today's tone.) | SeekingAlpha: neutral (Macro commentary is mixed; no strong risk-on/off consensus.). News tone adjusts bias confidence but does not override session structure.

trade_suggestion.rationale: Daily Bias and US Open Bias are both Bearish, but confidence is moderate (0.53). There is some edge, but mixed conditions suggest reduced size.

accuracy.explanation: Daily Bias was Bearish and the market closed Bearish. The bias correctly anticipated the full-day direction.
